steps.title: Spot the progression from broad to specific
steps.content: The passage moves from announcing a broad topic—researchers' general interest in shipwrecks and their ecological effects—to describing one specific study (Moseley's team) investigating that topic, to reporting one concrete finding. This general-to-specific progression is the passage's organizational backbone.

steps.title: Match the progression to the answer choices
steps.content: Choice C directly captures this three-part movement: it names the general scientific interest first, then the specific study, then one finding from it. The other three misidentify the passage's structure by either inventing details not present or mischaracterizing what's discussed. That matches choice C — C is correct.
